pr.title: Library, menu, and game system decompilation matches
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Closed, unmerged PR that replaced placeholders with library/menu/game/trophy decomp implementations and typed prototypes. The PR body claimed 8 matched functions, but the decomp.dev bot corroborated 5 new 100% function matches and several partial/data improvements: matched code rose to 61.13% (+0.03%, +1120 bytes). No human review comments or closing rationale are present in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented `lbArq_80014D2C`, initializing the ARQ global lists by clearing list heads, making `list[0]` point at the node pool in `lbArq_804316C0`, linking nodes as a free list, zeroing states, and using `PAD_STACK(8)`. Bot reported this as a new 100% match (+248 bytes).

Implemented `mnNameNew_8023B224(u8)`, handling exit from the name-entry screen based on `gm_801A4310()` context, including SisLib/menu cleanup, `gm_80190FE4`, `mnName_8023A9B4`, and `mnCharSel_802640A0` transitions. Bot reported this as a new 100% match (+240 bytes).

Implemented `fn_800244F4(void)`, resetting many `lbaudio_ax` volume, pitch, flag, and effect-state globals to defaults such as `0x7F`, `1.0F`, `0`, and `-1`, then calling `gm_801603B0()`. The PR also added missing static/extern declarations and a typed header prototype. Bot reported this as a new 100% match (+224 bytes).

Implemented `fn_8001EB14(THPDecComp*, const char*)`, calling `THPInit`, converting a movie path to a DVD entry number, reading the first `0x40` bytes through `lbFile_800161C4`, copying THP header fields into component fields, emitting `OSReport` warnings for unsupported frame offsets/newer file formats, and setting decoder state flags. Bot reported this as a new 100% match (+220 bytes).

Implemented `un_80307828(int)`, resetting trophy viewer camera/light state, restoring camera interest from `un_803B8858`, setting zoom-like field `x20` to `38.0F`, and adding missing `TyCameraData_` fields. Bot reported this as a new 100% match (+188 bytes).

Added near-matching implementations for `gm_80164504`, `fn_8024FC48`, and `mn_802307F8`: stage unlock notification/bitmask update, Data Delete menu exit/highlight handling, and Rules menu text selection/display respectively. The bot listed them as improvements, not full matches: 99.52%, 98.26%, and 85.89%.

Expanded headers and local structs to support field-accurate access: `THPDecComp` gained header/state fields at explicit offsets, `struct mn_802307F8_t` gained a `HSD_Text*` at `0x130`, `TyCameraData_` gained camera reset fields through `x58`, and several function declarations moved from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to typed prototypes.
The bot also reported related data-segment movement, including `.data` improvement in `lbmthp`, `.sdata`/`.sdata2` improvements in `lbaudio_ax`, `.sdata2` in `mnmainrule`, and `.sdata2` in `toy`.
smart_moves: The PR updated public headers and static headers alongside C implementations, so newly inferred signatures and globals were usable by callers instead of remaining isolated local guesses.
Struct expansion was offset-driven and conservative: fields remained `unk_XX` or `xNN` where semantics were unclear, while padding preserved layout.

Match-sensitive code preserved low-level control-flow and codegen cues such as `goto found_stage/found_notify`, explicit casts, exact integer/float constants, chained assignment, and `PAD_STACK` calls.

The THP opener used the component buffer itself as the destination for the `0x40` byte header read, then mapped header offsets into named width/height/state fields, matching the observed loader behavior without overnaming every header word.

The audio reset implementation gathered many adjacent static/global state variables into one deterministic reset routine and added the missing `lbaudio_ax.static.h` labels needed for both code and data matching.
The decomp.dev bot output was useful evidence: it separated true 100% function matches from sub-100 attempts despite the PR body's broader '8 matched functions' wording.
decomp_lessons: Do not trust PR-body match counts alone; compare them against decomp.dev itemized output. Here, 5 functions were confirmed 100%, while 3 attempted functions remained partial.

For menu decompilation, byte-level `user_data` offsets, global menu state checks, SisLib text cleanup, and GObj proc setup often need to be represented directly before a cleaner struct model is known.
Unknown struct fields with exact offset comments are preferable to speculative semantic names when the implementation needs layout accuracy but field meaning is not yet certain.
Data-segment matching can require declaring or reclassifying static labels and constants in `.static.h`/headers, not only writing the function body.

Near matches like `gm_80164504`, `fn_8024FC48`, and `mn_802307F8` are good future comparison targets because their remaining gaps are likely in fine-grained control flow, type width, constants, or struct typing.
Closed-unmerged PRs can still be valuable as scratch evidence, but final decomp work should reconcile them with later accepted code before reusing signatures or field names.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address-based project names such as `fn_800244F4`, `fn_8001EB14`, `fn_8024FC48`, `mn_802307F8`, `un_80307828`, and `gm_80164504`.
Subsystem prefixes were preserved: `lbArq_` for ARQ library code, `lbAudioAx_` for audio, `mnNameNew_`/`mn_` for menu code, `gm_` for game-mode code, and `un_` in the trophy/toy area.
Headers use address comments next to prototypes, for example `/* 0244F4 */ void fn_800244F4(void);`.
Unknown fields and padding follow existing conventions such as `unk_08`, `unk_128`, `x58`, `pad30`, and offset comments inside structs.
Unidentified linker/data labels were kept as `lbl_...`, such as `lbl_804D63F8` and `lbl_803B790C`, rather than being renamed without strong evidence.
A function-scoped inferred struct name, `struct mn_802307F8_t`, was used where the layout was only known for that menu helper.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(8)` was used in `lbArq_80014D2C` and `mnNameNew_8023B224`; `PAD_STACK(12)` was used in `un_80307828`.
`gm_80164504` used explicit labels and gotos (`found_stage`, `found_notify`) plus `(s32)` casts around `u8` comparisons and `1LL << (s32) unlock_idx` for the unlock bitmask attempt.
`un_80307828` used a local `Vec3 interest` copy from `un_803B8858`, exact `0.0F`/`38.0F` constants, and chained assignment `data->x18 = data->x1C = (f32)(data->x58 = 0)`.
`fn_8001EB14` depended on precise struct offsets in `THPDecComp` for header words at `0x08`, `0x0C`, `0x10`, `0x14`, `0x1C`, and `0x24`, plus state fields through `0x134`.
`mn_802307F8` used exact SisLib float constants (`-9.5F`, `8.0F`, `17.0F`, `364.68332F`, `76.77544F`, `0.0521F`) and direct indexing into `mn_803EC818`.

`lbaudio_ax.static.h` additions show a data-matching tactic: declare missing `sdata` globals such as `lbl_804D63F8`, `lbl_804D3890`, `lbl_804D3894`, `lbl_804D389C`, and `lbl_804D38A4` so assignments land in the expected storage.
review_feedback: No human reviews or inline review comments are present in the slice.
The only comment is from `decomp-dev[bot]`: GALE01 `0795d2b - 270fe2d`, matched code 61.13% (+0.03%, +1120 bytes).
Bot confirmed 5 new 100% function matches: `lbArq_80014D2C`, `mnNameNew_8023B224`, `fn_800244F4`, `fn_8001EB14`, and `un_80307828`.
Bot listed `gm_80164504`, `fn_8024FC48`, and `mn_802307F8` as unmatched-item improvements at 99.52%, 98.26%, and 85.89%, so the title/body should be interpreted cautiously.
PR metadata shows it was closed without merge; the available comments do not explain why.
searchable_terms: Library menu game system decompilation matches
OSReport Warning frame offsets not supported
follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs or current master implementations for `lbArq_80014D2C`, `fn_800244F4`, `fn_8001EB14`, `mnNameNew_8023B224`, and `un_80307828` to confirm final accepted code and naming.
Search for `gm_80164504` follow-up work; compare why this attempt stayed at 99.52%, including return type, branch shape, and stage unlock bitmask codegen.
Search for `fn_8024FC48` later implementations; inspect whether `user_data` indexing, `HSD_GObjProc` flags, or `mnDataDel_803EF870` typing caused the 98.26% gap.
Search for `mn_802307F8` later implementations; verify the inferred `struct mn_802307F8_t`, SisLib constants, and `mn_803EC818` indexing behind the 85.89% result.
Audit later `lbmthp.h` changes to refine `THPDecComp` field names against THP header semantics without losing offset accuracy.
Look outside this slice for maintainer or author comments explaining why PR #2367 was closed unmerged.
